source: CIS AWS COMPUTE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1.1.0
title: Ensure your Windows Server based lightsail instances are updated with the latest security patches.

input_row.source: CIS AWS COMPUTE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1.1.0
input_row.title: Ensure your Windows Server based lightsail instances are updated with the latest security patches.
input_row.description: Windows server based Lightsail instances are still managed by the consumer and any security updates or patches have to be installed and maintained by the user.
input_row.rationale: Windows Server-based Lightsail instances need to be updated with the latest security patches so they are not vulnerable to attacks. Be sure your server is configured to download and install updates.
input_row.audit: From the Console: 1. Login to AWS Console using https://console.aws.amazon.com 2. Click All services, click Lightsail under Compute. 3. This will open up the Lightsail console. 4. Select the Windows Instance you want to review. 5. Make sure the instance status is running. 6. Connect to the instance using Connect using RDP. 7. Log in using the credentials you have set for this instance. 8. Open a command prompt 9. Type sconfig, and then press Enter. Windows Update Settings are at number 5 and by default are set to Automatic. If this is the current setting continue with step 10. If this is not the current setting refer to the remediation below and start at step 10. 10. To determine if any updates are required, type 6, and then press Enter. 11. Type A to search for (A)ll updates in the new command window, and then press Enter. If any updates are required refer to the remediation below and start at step 14.
input_row.remediation: From the Console: 1. Login to AWS Console using https://console.aws.amazon.com 2. Click All services, click Lightsail under Compute. 3. This will open up the Lightsail console. 4. Select the Windows Instance you want to review. 5. Make sure the instance status is running. 6. Connect to the instance using Connect using RDP. 7. Log in using the credentials you have set for this instance. 8. Open a command prompt 9. Type sconfig, and then press Enter. Windows Update Settings are at number 5 and by default are set to Automatic. If this is not the current setting continue with step 10. If this is the current setting skip to step 12 10. Type 5, and then press Enter. 11. Type A for Automatic and then press Enter. Wait until the setting is saved and you return back to the server configuration menu. 12. Type 6, and then press Enter. 13. Type A to search for (A)ll updates in the new command window, and then press Enter. 14. Type A again to install (A)ll updates, and then press Enter. When finished, you see a message with the installation results and more instructions (if those apply).
input_row.justification: Both AI opinions correctly identify that verifying and updating Windows Server-based Lightsail instances for security patches requires interactive RDP access to the operating system. AWS does not expose Windows Update status or configuration for Lightsail instances via any API, CLI, or SDK. The audit and remediation steps explicitly require manual inspection and configuration within the Windows OS, which cannot be automated using AWS-native tools.
input_row.manual_summary: 1. Log in to the AWS Management Console and navigate to the Lightsail service. 2. Select the target Windows Server-based Lightsail instance and ensure it is running. 3. Connect to the instance using the "Connect using RDP" option in the Lightsail console. Use the appropriate credentials. 4. Once logged in to the Windows Server, open the Command Prompt. 5. Type `sconfig` and press Enter to launch the Server Configuration tool. 6. Select option 5 (Windows Update Settings) to review and configure automatic updates. 7. Check for and install any pending security updates using the tool. 8. Confirm that the instance is configured to automatically download and install updates, and that all critical security patches are applied. 9. Document the update status and configuration for audit purposes.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**APPROACH DECISION:**
The control requires verifying and updating Windows Server-based Lightsail instances for security patches, which necessitates interactive RDP access to the operating system. There are no AWS APIs or CLI commands that can check the Windows Update status or configuration for Lightsail instances. Additionally, the audit and remediation steps explicitly involve manual inspection and configuration within the Windows OS, making automation unfeasible. 

**MANUAL STEPS:**
1. Log in to the AWS Management Console and navigate to the Lightsail service.
2. Select the target Windows Server-based Lightsail instance and ensure it is in the “running” state.
3. Click the “Connect using RDP” option in the Lightsail console, and use the appropriate credentials for access.
4. Once logged into the Windows Server, open the Command Prompt.
5. Type `sconfig` and press Enter to launch the Server Configuration tool.
6. Select option 5 (Windows Update Settings) to review and ensure automatic updates are configured correctly.
7. Check for and install any pending security updates by selecting option 6, then typing A to search for all updates, and pressing Enter.
8. If updates are required, type A again to install all updates, then press Enter.
9. Confirm that the instance is configured to automatically download and install updates and that all critical security patches are applied.
10. Document the update status and configuration for audit purposes.
